title: client_ip gratuitous .to_string() in hot inbox path
description:
LOCATION: src/server.rs:756-763

FINDING: client_ip is built with .to_string() on the trimmed header value and then passed to try_acquire(&client_ip). RateLimiter::try_acquire takes &str, so the String is constructed only to immediately borrow it. The allocation happens on every inbound request to both inbox endpoints.

IMPACT: One unnecessary heap allocation per inbox request. Under sustained load (e.g. a fediverse storm) this adds GC pressure. The fix is trivial: remove the .to_string() and let the borrow live through the try_acquire call, using a let binding for the trimmed value.
close_reason: Documented: comment explains why .to_string() is required
